title: Firebase App Distribution
objectives: Set up App Distribution for daily internal builds

sections.markdown: **Firebase App Distribution** ships pre-release APK/AAB/IPA builds directly to invited testers by email — no store review. Perfect for daily internal builds where feedback loops matter more than scale.

sections.code:
# From CI (GitHub Actions / Codemagic / Fastlane)
firebase appdistribution:distribute app-release.apk \
  --app 1:1234:android:abcd \
  --release-notes "$(git log -1 --pretty=%B)" \
  --groups qa,internal

sections.markdown: Typical pipeline stages: **App Distribution** (nightly, internal), **Play Internal / TestFlight** (weekly, wider beta), **Production** (release). Each stage widens the audience and slows the cadence. App Distribution is the fastest feedback tier.
Include commit SHA + author in release notes so testers know what changed. Use the Firebase App Distribution SDK for an in-app 'new build available' prompt.

flashcards.front: App Distribution vs Play Internal / TestFlight?
flashcards.back: Fastest / smallest / no review — for daily internal QA. The others are for wider beta with more infrastructure.
firebase CLI + Fastlane plugin + GitHub Actions action; all support tester groups + release notes.

options: No review — direct to invited testers

quiz.q: Best place in a release pipeline for App Distribution?
quiz.explain: It's optimized for fast internal loops.

challenges.text: Upload one APK to a tester group via CLI.
Automate uploads from GitHub Actions on push to main.
Add in-app update prompt via Testers SDK.
Auto-tag PR builds and post the App Distribution link on the PR.

interviewQuestions: When use App Distribution vs Play Internal vs TestFlight?
How do you deprecate old builds?
